failure_surface: The output file failed validation despite successful code execution. The agent deleted matching rows but the final result did not meet evaluation criteria.
root_cause: Two primary issues: (1) The row-deletion logic was imprecise - deleting any rows that participated in a match rather than strictly adjacent pairs as implied by 'row pairs'. (2) Using pandas.to_excel() instead of openpyxl caused loss of formatting and potentially formulas, violating data integrity preservation requirements. Additionally, initial syntax errors due to malformed code blocks delayed successful execution.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: When deleting rows based on matching conditions, ensure strict pair-wise deletion logic that verifies rows form proper pairs per task specification. Always use openpyxl for .xlsx writing tasks to preserve formatting and formulas, never pandas.to_excel alone.
evidence: Agent initially received SyntaxError on lines starting with 'python' due to improperly formatted code blocks
Final execution ran successfully but test_case_results was [0] indicating evaluation failure
Code used df_filtered.to_excel() directly instead of openpyxl Workbook approach
Matching logic allowed non-paired deletions by adding all matching indices without verifying true pair relationships
Instruction explicitly mentioned 'row pairs' suggesting adjacency or strict pairing requirement was not enforced

memory_items.title: Row Pair Deletion Requires Adjacency Verification
memory_items.lesson: When instructions specify 'row pairs' or similar grouping language, verify the logical relationship between matched rows before deletion.
memory_items.generalization: Row filtering logic should validate that deleted rows satisfy the pairing condition explicitly, not just membership in a match set.

memory_items.title: Use openpyxl for Writing Formatted Excel Files
memory_items.lesson: pandas.to_excel() does not preserve cell formatting, merged cells, number formats, or formulas. Use openpyxl's Workbook objects when these must be retained.
memory_items.generalization: For .xlsx output files requiring format preservation, load with openpyxl, modify the worksheet object directly, then save.

memory_items.title: Ensure Proper Code Block Formatting in Execution Environments
memory_items.lesson: Markdown code block delimiters or special characters can cause syntax errors if injected into code incorrectly.
memory_items.generalization: Validate that generated code is clean and executable before submission; avoid wrapping code with unintended characters or markers.

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Row Pair Operations
When task specifies 'pairs' or grouped row operations, validate pairing constraints beyond simple matching. Verify rows meet adjacency or explicit relationship criteria before deletion.

### Output Format Preservation
Always use openpyxl for writing .xlsx files when formatting matters. pandas.to_excel() converts formulas to values and strips all cell styles.

### Code Submission Hygiene
Ensure generated code blocks are syntactically clean. Validate no stray characters, incomplete delimiters, or environment-specific markers appear in submitted code.
